description: objectArrayLayout option - mixed columnar encoding for object arrays with primitive and complex fields

tests.name: columnar: all-primitive objects produce same output as tabular
tests.note: When all fields are primitive, columnar and tabular produce identical output

tests.name: columnar: primitive and object fields produce spill rows
tests.expected:
[2]{id,name}:
  1,Alice
    address:
      city: NY
  2,Bob
    address:
      city: LA

tests.name: columnar: primitive and array fields produce spill rows
columnar: mixed primitive, object, and array spill fields
tests.expected:
[2]{id,name,score}:
  1,Alice,9.5
    tags[2]: a,b
    address:
      city: NY
  2,Bob,7.0
    tags[1]: c
    address:
      city: LA

tests.name: columnar: row with no spill fields has no spill lines
tests.note: Row 1 has no complex fields and emits no spill lines; row 2 has address and emits a spill block

tests.name: columnar: nested columnar array as spill field
tests.note: Nested object arrays in spill blocks use the columnar form recursively when their elements are uniform primitives

tests.name: auto: non-uniform objects fall back to expanded list
tests.expected:
[2]:
  - id: 1
    name: Alice
  - id: 2
    name: Bob
    extra: true
tests.note: auto mode: non-uniform keys prevent tabular form, fall back to expanded list

tests.name: auto: mixed-value objects fall back to expanded list
tests.expected:
[2]:
  - id: 1
    name: Alice
    address:
      city: NY
  - id: 2
    name: Bob
    address:
      city: LA
tests.note: auto mode: complex fields prevent tabular detection, fall back to expanded list

tests.name: columnar: root array (no key) with spill rows
tests.note: Root columnar array omits the key, uses [N]{fields}: header form
